channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Why senior devs quit FAANG in 2026
item.description: By the end of 2025, a 2024 Stack Overflow survey found that 38% of developers with 3–5 years of experience had left a company within two years — not for…

item.title: AI salary boosters: prompt vs fine-tuning in 2026
item.description: Fine-tuning beats prompt engineering 15 to 7 for accuracy and cuts API bills 75% — here’s the data and the stack you need to prove it.

item.title: AI code hides 5 deadly permissions
item.description: Catch AI-generated security flaws in 15 minutes — 5 traps teams miss, real PR examples, and the exact Semgrep rules to block them before merge

item.title: Senior exodus: why scale kills joy
item.description: Cut on-call pages 70% with connection pooling and timeouts — the real reasons seniors leave big tech and how to fix them

item.title: Anchor remote pay to global rates
item.description: Close remote salary gaps 21% with Levels.fyi data — Python 3.11 scripts, ObservableHQ dashboard, and email templates that shift the negotiation from cost to market rate.

item.title: Database pools are too small by default
item.description: Cut database connection timeouts and outages 80% by abandoning the default pool size. Learn how to set max pool size, timeouts, and budgets that actually work in 2026.

item.title: 2026 AI salaries: which skills pay
item.description: Cut AI salary stagnation in 2026: prompt observability vs fine-tuning salaries compared with real data, benchmarks, and cost figures.

item.title: Compare WebSockets, SSE, long polling
item.description: Cut 30 % of realtime costs with the right transport — p95 latency, CPU, and memory at 2 k concurrent users on Node 20 + Redis 7.2 in 2026

item.title: Ship real projects, land remote dev jobs
item.description: Land remote jobs from Africa with one signal: production-grade GitHub repos. See real examples, benchmarks, and trade-offs that moved the needle in 2026.

item.title: RAG in prod: 3 silent failures
item.description: RAG pipelines fail under load — here’s what breaks first: FAISS isn’t thread-safe, batch_size lies under concurrency, and Redis cache isn’t enough. Learn how we cut latency 63% and errors from 8% to 0.2% with Weaviate 1.28 and isolated workers.

item.title: Your pool size is killing throughput
item.description: Cut API response time 40% by fixing connection pool size — the outdated CPU-core rule wastes RAM and adds latency. Learn how to size pools based on concurrency demand and max connections.

item.title: Real-time showdown: WebSocket vs SSE vs long polling
item.description: Pick the realtime transport that won’t collapse under load — 2026 benchmarks for WebSockets, SSE, and long polling with concrete latency, memory, and cost numbers.

item.title: AI pay split: fine-tune vs prompt in 2026
item.description: AI fine-tuning vs prompt engineering in 2026: 14 % accuracy uplift, 28 ms latency, and $1 200 compute cost — the data-backed salary split every engineer should know

item.title: Ship a system, not a profile
item.description: Ship systems, not projects, to land remote jobs from Africa — benchmarks, cost data, and post-mortems that actually move the needle. 2026 hiring reality.

item.title: Win remote pay talks with Payscale data
item.description: Their offer was $65,000; my market research said $95,000 for the same role and level in New York.

item.title: Senior devs flee big tech: hidden reasons
item.description: 18–22% annual attrition among senior engineers at top tech giants in 2026 despite $210k–$280k salaries. The hidden causes are autonomy loss, approval overload, and on-call burnout — not money.

item.title: FastAPI vs Django REST costs revealed
item.description: FastAPI vs Django REST latency, cost, and hiring trade-offs in 2026 — measured on C6i.large with Redis 7.2 and Python 3.11. Pick the wrong stack and you’ll burn $216/month or debug async leaks at 3 a.m.

item.title: Ship production-grade portfolios from Africa
item.description: Build a portfolio that proves you can ship production code under constraints — not just write code. Includes real examples, benchmarks, and cost breakdowns from African devs who got hired remotely in 2026.

item.title: Choose the right push tech fast
item.description: Pick the right push tech fast — benchmark WebSockets vs SSE vs polling on latency, CPU, and bytes sent for 8,000 updates/sec in 2026

item.title: Leaky RAG: 3 prod traps tutorials ignore
item.description: Cut RAG pipeline latency 77% and cloud bill 64% with Qdrant, Redis, and a reranker layer — the operating details most tutorials skip and the exact configs that worked in production.
